tldr: **Crumbs PR #1 merged ✓** — social meta + OG image now on `v2-identity-card` (commit `66ccce6`). Railway redeploys within a minute. [Validate the unfurl →](https://www.opengraph.xyz/url/https%3A%2F%2Fcrumbs-production.up.railway.app%2F) once it's live. One open question remains: personal-site scaffold (R002).
tldr_short: Crumbs PR #1 merged. Railway redeploying. Personal-site scaffold still blocked on R002.

items.title: Read the multi-agent LLM paper
items.desc: Orogat et al. benchmark 5 multi-agent frameworks. Extract their evaluation axes — compare against MAE's 29 evaluators.

items.title: Pull one real eval case
items.desc: Use eval frontend to fetch a failing case, run it locally, see what the evaluators actually score.

items.title: Touch one side project
items.desc: Crumbs v2 identity-card is closest to ship. Even 30 min of polish counts.

items.title: Papers
items.subtitle: Deep briefs with code, figures, and next-step checklists.

items.title: Learning · MAE
items.subtitle: Knowledge map of the eval paradigm + 29 evaluators. Daytime agent fills this in.

items.title: Side projects
items.subtitle: Live status for Crumbs, personal site, Gestgo. Night builder opens draft PRs here.

items.title: Experiments
items.subtitle: Overnight results with full writeups — code, plots, conclusion.

paper.title: Understanding Multi-Agent LLM Frameworks: A Unified Benchmark and Experimental Analysis
paper.authors: Abdelghny Orogat, Ana Rostam, Essam Mansour
paper.tldr: First unified benchmark comparing multi-agent LLM frameworks on how their architecture (interaction, memory, coordination) changes downstream task performance.
paper.why: This is the closest published cousin of what MAE does. Their evaluation axes — **agent interaction, information storage, task coordination** — map almost 1-to-1 onto what your 29 evaluators try to score. Steal their framing for any eval PR you propose to Jun.
key_findings: Architecture choice (hierarchical vs peer-to-peer) affects pass rate more than model choice
Memory persistence across turns is the #1 predictor of multi-step task success
LLM-as-judge evaluation has high variance without task-conditioned rubrics

items.title: Google's 8th-gen TPU: two chips for the agentic era
items.summary: Google Cloud announces next-gen TPUs explicitly positioned for agent workloads — separate inference vs. orchestration silicon.
items.why: Confirms agentic inference is now an infra-differentiated market. Watch for MAE cost/latency numbers to shift.

items.title: SpaceX reportedly acquiring Cursor for $60B
items.summary: Rumored mega-deal: Cursor IDE + SpaceX autonomy stack. Dev-tool agents entering industrial automation territory.
items.why: Signals that 'coding agent' category is consolidating fast. Your side-project moat = personal data + taste, not model quality.

items.title: All your agents are going async
items.summary: Essay arguing multi-agent systems must move from sync request/response to async message passing for scale.
items.why: Directly relevant to MAE architecture — ask Jackson whether the eval harness currently models async delivery.

items.title: Real-time, self-evolving personal-agent persona
items.quote: I hope every personal agent could have a real-time persona… so I won't face tedious text to do brief everyday.

sections.title: Reflection & plan — v3
sections.text:
**What I heard:** Apple-style + minimalist. Important content deserves inner pages (blog/code/interactive viz). Agent should work during the day too, build side projects when idle, and the feedback loop should compound.

**Three redesigns, one direction:**

1. **Dashboard v3 — Apple shell + drill-downs.** Swap Linear dark for Apple tokens (alternating black / `#f5f5f7`, SF Pro, single blue accent, pill CTAs). Hub stays minimal — cards link to inner pages like `/papers/<slug>` where the agent pours depth (markdown + code + p5.js viz + arXiv embed).

2. **24/7 agent — 4 cron modes.** 🌅 Morning brief (07:00) · ☀️ Daytime learning (every 2h, 09–17 weekdays — reads MAE docs + evaluators, builds a knowledge map at `/learning/*`) · 🌙 Night builder (22:00 — drafts PRs on side projects, stops at review checkpoints) · 🔔 On-demand review. All write to a shared `agent-journal.jsonl` + `pending-review.json`.

3. **Compounding feedback.** `decisions.json` is the ADR log (seeded today with D001–D003). `/feedback` page surfaces pending questions as one-tap chips. Every answer becomes a typed decision — I never re-ask.

**Full doc:** [read the full proposal here](./proposal.html?doc=v3-apple-multipage-24-7) (no login needed) — rollout plan D+0 through D+7, safety rails, migration path, token budgets.

**Decisions I need from you → next section.**

items.title: v1 — static HTML brief
items.detail: Linear.app dark design, vision-scored 9/10 balance & scannability.

items.title: v2 — deployed to GitHub Pages
items.detail: Data-driven: `index.html` loads `data/latest.json`, renderer registry per section type. No more daily HTML writing.

items.title: v2.0.1 — hardened fetch
items.detail: 3× retry + 8s timeout + cache-bust + Retry button + auto-reload on reconnect. Fix for 'Load failed' Safari error.

items.title: v2.1 — 4 new section types
items.detail: Added **paper-brief**, **trends**, **review-points**, **overnight-log** renderers. Each is data-driven — no template edits needed tomorrow.

items.title: v2.2 — decisions.json seeded (D001–D003)
items.detail: Your feedback now lives in code, not memory. Paper = **mix 1 of each** (new + fit-to-MAE). Trends = **HN + Reddit + Product Hunt**. Review-points = **cap 3, min 1**. Agent loads this at every run — never re-asks.

items.title: v3 PROPOSAL drafted
items.detail: Apple-minimal redesign + multi-page drill-downs (`/papers/*`, `/learning/*`, `/projects/*`, `/experiments/*`, `/feedback`) + 24/7 agent workflow (4 modes via cron) + compounding feedback loop. See proposal doc below.

items.title: v2.3 — proposals readable on the live site
items.detail: New `/proposal.html` page renders any markdown under `data/proposals/*.md` with a clean typographic style. Index page at `/proposal.html` lists all proposals. Accessible from your phone without GitHub login.

items.title: Locked D004–D009 in decisions.json
items.detail: Your answers to Q1–Q6 are now typed decisions. Theme = **auto-OS + toggle**. Inner pages v1 = **papers + learning + projects + experiments**. Daytime = **12:00 & 17:00 + idle-trigger**. Night builder = **draft PRs freely**. Crons = **laptop primary, cloud supplementary**. Budget = **no hard cap, ask on rate-limit**.

items.title: v3 design system (Apple tokens, auto-theme)
items.detail: Rewrote `assets/style.css` with Apple light-first tokens + dark variant. Header toggle persists to localStorage. Single blue accent `#0071e3` / `#2997ff`. SF Pro family. Pill CTAs 980px. All existing section types re-skinned without schema changes.

items.title: Inner-page framework (page.html + page-render.js)
items.detail: One generic shell reads `data/pages/<kind>/<slug>.json`. Block types: **prose, code, callout, quote, embed, checklist, gallery, html**. Checklists persist state to localStorage. Section-index page (`section.html?kind=…`) for listing.

items.title: 4 inner pages seeded with real content
items.detail: `/papers/…-orogat-multiagent-llm` (full paper brief + code snippet + reading checklist). `/learning/mae-eval-paradigm` (paradigm overview + evaluator backlog). `/projects/{crumbs-v2, personal-site, gestgo}` (status + roadmap + next actions). `/experiments/…-dashboard-v3` (this experiment's writeup).

items.title: /feedback page live
items.detail: Three tabs: **pending** (tap to pick answers), **decisions** (full ADR history), **quick note** (free-form, copy as chat reply). All state in localStorage for now — server-side commit-back ships with cron infra.

items.title: v3.1 — cron infra (launchd + GitHub Actions)
items.detail: Next: morning brief cron (07:00), daytime learning runs (12:00 + 17:00 + idle), night builder (22:00). Plus Actions fallback for always-on. ETA: same-day continuation.

items.title: v3.1 — cron infra installed on your laptop
items.detail: Three launchd jobs loaded in `~/Library/LaunchAgents/`: morning (07:00 daily), daytime (weekdays 12:00 & 17:00), night (22:00 daily). Heartbeat verified: `data/agent-journal.jsonl` now has a daytime entry. Manage with `scripts/launchd/install.sh {install|uninstall|status}`.

items.title: GitHub Actions fallback
items.detail: `.github/workflows/morning-brief.yml` runs at 07:15 Asia/Shanghai — if your laptop was asleep at 07:00 local, the brief still regenerates and pushes. `workflow_dispatch` lets you trigger on-demand via `gh workflow run morning-brief.yml`.

items.title: Next: replace mode stubs with real agent runs
items.detail: Current `run-mode.sh` logs a heartbeat per mode. Next iteration wires each mode to a proper agent prompt (morning: author the brief JSON; daytime: pick 1 evaluator and write its `/learning/` page; night: open a draft PR on Crumbs). Posting a reflection + Q&A in tomorrow's brief.

items.title: Night-builder started on Crumbs v2
items.detail: Inspected repo; earlier notes (typography flat, mobile <360px broken) were guesses. Real gap: **zero OG/Twitter meta tags** — links unfurl blank in Feishu/Twitter/WeChat. Scoped PR to social meta + OG image only. Skipped speculative icon/favicon/manifest — flagged for your review.

items.title: Built 1200×630 branded OG image
items.detail: Ink bg + Fraunces yellow wordmark + pink *you* accent + pink chip + URL. Rendered via headless Chrome, vision-reviewed for clipping/contrast, shippable.

items.title: Draft PR #1 opened
items.detail: `polish/social-meta-tags` → `v2-identity-card`. Full OG/Twitter/WeChat/theme-color meta set. `npm run build` green (354 KB js, 13 KB css, 179ms). **Not merged** — draft, waiting for you.

items.title: Queued next work while you review
items.detail: Planned: personal-site `/now` page (scope: single route, 1–2 paragraphs of current focus, links to Crumbs + morning-brief). Will start after this morning-brief push lands.

items.title: Personal-site work paused — needs your call
items.detail: BedRock repo is docs/context only, not a web scaffold. Bootstrapping a site = stack + domain + tone decisions = critical-scope per D007. Stopping instead of guessing. See review item **R002** in /feedback.

items.title: Crumbs PR #1 merged (user approved)
items.detail: Merged via squash, branch deleted. Commit `66ccce6` now on `v2-identity-card`. Decision logged as D010. Railway auto-deploys from that branch — OG preview goes live within ~60s.

items.question: Personal-site — how should I bootstrap it?
items.context: BedRock repo is docs/context only. No web scaffold anywhere. Pick one and I'll ship the first PR next run.
